metadata.notes: Includes handling of physical objects (box, bags) and interaction with external entities (TransportCompany, delivery person). Describes a manual fallback process for system downtime.

inputs.vague: The Receiving Agent receives a box from the transport company, validates its ID, and then records its arrival into the system with relevant details including the number of bags inside. Finally, the agent removes the bags and gives them to the Registration Operator.
inputs.detailed:
UC MO1. Register arrival of a box
RA means "Receiving Agent".
RO means "Registration Operator"
DS means "Department Supervisor"
Primary Actor: RA
System: Nightime Receiving Registry Software
1. RA receives and opens box (box id, bags with bag ids) from TransportCompany TC
2. RA validates box id with TC registered ids.
3. RA maybe signs paper form for delivery person
4. RA registers arrival into system, which stores:
RA id
date, time
box id
TransportCompany
<Person name?>
# bags (?with bag ids)
<estimated value?>
5. RA removes bags from box, puts onto cart, takes to RO.
Extensions:
2a. box id does not match transport company
4a. fire alarm goes off and interrupts registration
4b. computer goes down
leave the money on the desk and wait for computer to come back up.

groundTruth.name: UC MO1. Register arrival of a box
groundTruth.summary: The Receiving Agent registers the arrival of a box containing bags into the system, validating its ID and ensuring proper transfer to the Registration Operator.

steps.description: Receives and opens box (with box ID and bags with bag IDs) from Transport Company.
Validates box ID against Transport Company's registered IDs.
Signs paper form for delivery person (optional).
Registers arrival into the System, providing RA ID, date, time, box ID, Transport Company, number of bags, and estimated value if available.
Removes bags from the box, places them onto a cart, and takes the cart to the Registration Operator.
Notifies the Transport Company and follows company protocol for unmatching box IDs.
Notifies the Transport Company and follows company protocol for unmatching box IDs.
Informs Transport Company about the invalid box ID and follows protocol for discrepancies.
Contact Transport Company for clarification and resolution.
Follows established protocol for handling invalid box IDs.
Notifies Transport Company of the mismatch and follows the defined protocol for such discrepancies.
Informs Transport Company about the mismatching box ID.
Follows internal protocol for handling invalid box IDs, potentially returning the box or seeking further instructions.
Contacts the Transport Company to resolve the box ID mismatch.
Follows company policy regarding unmatching box IDs.
Ceases registration process due to fire alarm.
Leaves all materials on the desk.
Waits for the computer system to become available again to resume registration.
